summary: AI and machine learning engineer with hands-on experience building and deploying production ML pipelines, containerized model serving with Docker and Kubernetes, and end-to-end ML Ops workflows. Proficient in Python, SQL, XGBoost, and PyTest, with practical experience optimizing models from training through deployment. Eager to contribute to Target's personalized recommendations platform by designing maintainable, well-tested, and scalable machine learning solutions.

skills.ml: XGBoost, DistilBERT, HuggingFace, LangChain, RAG, YOLO, scikit-learn, PyTorch, OpenAI API, Anthropic Claude API
skills.infra: Docker, Kubernetes, GitHub Actions, CI/CD, Railway, Supabase (PostgreSQL), Vercel, GCP, REST APIs

experience.title: Deep Learning Intern
bullets: Containerized a YOLO-based object detection model with Docker and deployed on Kubernetes for horizontal scaling of model inference, enabling the team to handle variable production load without code changes.

Built a data augmentation and preprocessing pipeline using NumPy and Pandas, scaling the training dataset from a few hundred to 10,000+ images and manually re-annotating 500+ edge cases; model accuracy improved from 65% to 85.4% on held-out evaluation data.

Developed the YOLO-based object detection model in Python to detect and classify industrial pipe inventory from warehouse images, improving accuracy by 30% and eliminating a fully manual counting process.
Designed normalized relational database schemas in SQL to store image metadata and inference results, enabling cross-team queries on model performance and inventory trends.

projects.name: AI Interview Engine
projects.subtitle: Voice-First Candidate Screening Platform
projects.stack: Next.js, Express, Claude Sonnet/Haiku, Deepgram, Supabase, Railway, Vercel
bullets: Built an automated document ingestion pipeline (PDF parsing, Claude Haiku, MD5 deduplication caching) and a post-interview evaluation system scoring candidates across 4 dimensions with advance/hold/reject output; deployed full-stack on Railway, Vercel, and Supabase (PostgreSQL with Row-Level Security and Magic Link authentication) with Brevo SMTP for transactional email automation.

Engineered a real-time voice pipeline integrating Deepgram Nova-2 (speech-to-text) and Aura (text-to-speech) with Claude Sonnet 4.6, achieving 4-5s end-to-end latency per conversational turn and replacing human interviewers for initial candidate screening.

Designed a multi-turn conversational AI engine with dynamic context injection: the LLM receives each candidate's parsed resume before the session and generates targeted, role-specific follow-up questions in real time.

projects.name: CodeSyntax AI
projects.subtitle: Full-Stack RAG Developer Assistant
projects.stack: Python, FastAPI, Next.js, LangChain, Pinecone, Docker, GitHub Actions
bullets: Maintained 35+ pytest unit tests and a 55-question domain evaluation set; GitHub Actions CI/CD pipeline builds the Docker image and deploys to Railway (API) and Vercel (frontend) on every merge to main.

Built a production retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) system using GPT-4o-mini with semantic search over Python Docs, RealPython, and StackOverflow, indexed in Pinecone via OpenAI vector embeddings.

Implemented an LLM-based query intent classifier and result re-ranker by source authority, plus multi-turn conversation support via LangChain chat-history condensing, both measurably reducing off-topic responses.

projects.name: AuthentiText
projects.subtitle: AI-Generated Text Detection Engine
projects.stack: Python, XGBoost, DistilBERT, HuggingFace, GCP, Docker
bullets: Trained a weighted ensemble model combining XGBoost (21 hand-crafted linguistic features: perplexity, burstiness, lexical density) with a fine-tuned DistilBERT transformer, achieving 99.1% F1 and 99.96% AUROC on held-out test data.

Conducted systematic model comparison across four architectures (Logistic Regression, Random Forest, XGBoost, DistilBERT) using ROC curves, confusion matrices, and SHAP feature importance; integrated Captum token-level attribution heatmaps for explainable AI (XAI).
Trained on the merged HC3 corpus spanning Wikipedia, Reddit, medicine, and finance domains; deployed as a publicly accessible machine learning inference app on HuggingFace Spaces.
